get.summary: Search project code using natural language
get.description:
Introduced in GitLab 18.11.

Searches indexed project code using semantic (meaning-based) similarity rather than
keyword matching. Use this when you do not know the exact symbol or file name, or
to discover how a behavior is implemented across the codebase.

Primary use cases:
- When you do not know the exact symbol or file path
- To see how a behavior or feature is implemented across the codebase
- To discover related implementations (clients, jobs, workers)

How to use:
- Provide a concise, specific query with concrete keywords
- Use directory_path to narrow scope (e.g. "app/services/")
- Prefer precise intent over broad terms

Results are grouped by file. Each file includes merged line ranges with content
and a relevance score (0.0-1.0). The response includes an overall confidence
level (high/medium/low/unknown) based on score distribution. Results are
filtered by Duo context exclusion settings.

Requires semantic code search to be enabled and indexed for the project namespace.

404.description: Not found - project not found or semantic code search unavailable

422.description: Unprocessable entity - project has no embeddings

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or URL-encoded path of the project

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Natural language search query (e.g. "authentication middleware", "rate limiting logic")

parameters.name: directory_path
parameters.description: Restrict search to files under this directory path (e.g. "app/services/"). Must be a relative path — no leading slash, no .. segments.

parameters.name: knn
parameters.description: Number of nearest neighbours to retrieve internally. Higher values improve recall at the cost of latency.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

get.summary: Get a list of project iterations
get.description: This feature was introduced in GitLab 13.5

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or URL-encoded path of the project

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Return "opened", "upcoming", "current (previously started)", "closed", or "all" iterations. Filtering by `started` state is deprecated starting with 14.1, please use `current` instead.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: The search criteria for the title of the iteration

parameters.name: in
parameters.description: Fields in which fuzzy search should be performed with the query given in the argument `search`. The available options are `title` and `cadence_title`. Defaults to `[title]`

parameters.name: include_ancestors
parameters.description: Include iterations from parent and its ancestors

parameters.name: include_descendants
parameters.description: Include iterations from parent and its descendants

parameters.name: updated_before
parameters.description: Return milestones updated before the specified datetime. Format: ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Return milestones updated after the specified datetime. Format: ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Current page number

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page

put.summary: Rollout namespaces to be indexed up to n%
put.description:
This feature was introduced in GitLab 12.7.

**DEPRECATED**: This endpoint is deprecated and for GitLab.com use only.

This will only ever increase the number of indexed namespaces. Providing a value lower than the current rolled out percentage will have no effect.

This percentage is never persisted but is used to calculate the number of new namespaces to rollout.

If the same percentage is applied again at a later time, due to possible new namespaces being created during the period, some of them will also be indexed. Therefore you may expect that setting this to 10%, then waiting a month and setting to 10% again will trigger new namespaces to be added (i.e. 10% of the number of newly created namespaces in the last month within the given plan).

put.summary: Rollback namespaces to be indexed down to n%
put.description:
This feature was introduced in GitLab 12.7.

**DEPRECATED**: This endpoint is deprecated and for GitLab.com use only.

This will only ever decrease the number of indexed namespaces. Providing a value higher than the current rolled out percentage will have no effect.

This percentage is never persisted but is used to calculate the number of namespaces to rollback.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Current page number

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: The name of a repository branch or tag, if not given the default branch is used

parameters.description: Return contributors ordered by `name` or `email` or `commits`

get.summary: Get the common ancestor between commits

get.summary: Generates a changelog section for a release and returns it
get.description: This feature was introduced in GitLab 14.6

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or URL-encoded path of the project

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The version of the release, using the semantic versioning format

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The first commit in the range of commits to use for the changelog

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The last commit in the range of commits to use for the changelog

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date and time of the release

parameters.name: trailer
parameters.description: The Git trailer to use for determining if commits are to be included in the changelog

parameters.name: config_file
parameters.description: The file path to the configuration file as stored in the project's Git repository. Defaults to '.gitlab/changelog_config.yml'

parameters.name: config_file_ref
parameters.description: The git reference (for example, branch) where the changelog configuration file is defined. Defaults to the default repository branch.

post.summary: Generates a changelog section for a release and commits it in a changelog file
post.description: This feature was introduced in GitLab 13.9

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or URL-encoded path of the project

get.summary: List Releases
get.description: Returns a paginated list of releases. This feature was introduced in GitLab 11.7.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or URL-encoded path of the project

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Current page number

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: The field to use as order. Either `released_at` (default) or `created_at`

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The direction of the order. Either `desc` (default) for descending order or `asc` for ascending order

parameters.name: include_html_description
parameters.description: If `true`, a response includes HTML rendered markdown of the release description

parameters.name: updated_before
parameters.description: Return releases updated before the specified datetime. Format: ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Return releases updated after the specified datetime. Format: ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

post.description: Creates a release. Developer level access to the project is required to create a release. This feature was introduced in GitLab 11.7.

parameters.name: id

parameters.name: tag_name
parameters.description: The Git tag the release is associated with

parameters.name: tag_name
parameters.description: The Git tag the release is associated with

get.summary: Get a user projects

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The ID or username of the user

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Return projects ordered by field. storage_size, repository_size, wiki_size, packages_size are only available to admins. Similarity is available when searching and is limited to projects the user has access to.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Return projects sorted in ascending and descending order

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Limit by archived status

parameters.name: visibility
parameters.description: Limit by visibility

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Return list of projects matching the search criteria

parameters.name: search_namespaces
parameters.description: Include ancestor namespaces when matching search criteria

parameters.name: owned
parameters.description: Limit by owned by authenticated user

parameters.name: starred
parameters.description: Limit by starred status

parameters.name: imported
parameters.description: Limit by imported by authenticated user

parameters.name: membership
parameters.description: Limit by projects that the current user is a member of

parameters.name: with_issues_enabled
parameters.description: Limit by enabled issues feature

parameters.name: with_merge_requests_enabled
parameters.description: Limit by enabled merge requests feature

parameters.name: with_programming_language
parameters.description: Limit to repositories which use the given programming language

parameters.name: min_access_level
parameters.description: Limit by minimum access level of authenticated user

parameters.name: id_after
parameters.description: Limit results to projects with IDs greater than the specified ID

parameters.name: id_before
parameters.description: Limit results to projects with IDs less than the specified ID

parameters.name: last_activity_after
parameters.description: Limit results to projects with last_activity after specified time. Format: ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: last_activity_before
parameters.description: Limit results to projects with last_activity before specified time. Format: ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: repository_storage
parameters.description: Which storage shard the repository is on. Available only to admins

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of topics. Limit results to projects having all topics

parameters.name: topic_id
parameters.description: Limit results to projects with the assigned topic given by the topic ID

parameters.name: updated_before
parameters.description: Return projects updated before the specified datetime. Format: ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Return projects updated after the specified datetime. Format: ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: include_pending_delete
parameters.description: Include projects in pending delete state. Can only be set by admins

parameters.name: marked_for_deletion_on
parameters.description: Date when the project was marked for deletion

parameters.description: Limit by projects that are not archived and not marked for deletion

parameters.name: wiki_checksum_failed
parameters.description: Limit by projects where wiki checksum is failed

parameters.name: repository_checksum_failed
parameters.description: Limit by projects where repository checksum is failed

parameters.name: include_hidden
parameters.description: Include hidden projects. Can only be set by admins

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Current page number

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page

parameters.name: simple
parameters.description: Return only the ID, URL, name, and path of each project

parameters.name: statistics
parameters.description: Include project statistics

parameters.description: Include custom attributes in the response

parameters.name: custom_attributes
parameters.description: Filter with custom attributes

get.summary: List merge requests
get.description: Get all merge requests the authenticated user has access to. By default it returns only merge requests created by the current user. To get all merge requests, use parameter `scope=all`.

parameters.name: author_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created by the given user `id`. Mutually exclusive with `author_username`. Combine with `scope=all` or `scope=assigned_to_me`.

parameters.name: author_username
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created by the given `username`. Mutually exclusive with `author_id`.

parameters.name: assignee_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests assigned to the given user `id`. `None` returns unassigned merge requests. `Any` returns merge requests with an assignee.

parameters.name: assignee_username
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created by the given `username`. Mutually exclusive with `author_id`.

parameters.name: reviewer_username
parameters.description: Returns merge requests which have the user as a reviewer with the given `username`. `None` returns merge requests with no reviewers. `Any` returns merge requests with any reviewer. Mutually exclusive with `reviewer_id`. Introduced in GitLab 13.8.

parameters.name: labels
parameters.description: Returns merge requests matching a comma-separated list of labels. `None` lists all merge requests with no labels. `Any` lists all merge requests with at least one label. Predefined names are case-insensitive.

parameters.name: milestone
parameters.description: Returns merge requests for a specific milestone. `None` returns merge requests with no milestone. `Any` returns merge requests that have an assigned milestone.

parameters.name: my_reaction_emoji
parameters.description: Returns merge requests reacted by the authenticated user by the given `emoji`. `None` returns issues not given a reaction. `Any` returns issues given at least one reaction.

parameters.name: reviewer_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests which have the user as a reviewer with the given user `id`. `None` returns merge requests with no reviewers. `Any` returns merge requests with any reviewer. Mutually exclusive with `reviewer_username`.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Returns `all` merge requests or just those that are `opened`, `closed`, `locked`, or `merged`.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Returns merge requests ordered by `created_at`, `label_priority`, `milestone_due`, `popularity`, `priority`, `title`, `updated_at` or `merged_at` fields. Introduced in GitLab 14.8.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Returns merge requests sorted in `asc` or `desc` order.

parameters.name: with_labels_details
parameters.description: If `true`, response returns more details for each label in labels field: `:name`,`:color`, `:description`, `:description_html`, `:text_color`

parameters.name: with_merge_status_recheck
parameters.description: If `true`, this projection requests (but does not guarantee) that the `merge_status` field be recalculated asynchronously. Introduced in GitLab 13.0.

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created on or after the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created on or before the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Returns merge requests updated on or after the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: updated_before
parameters.description: Returns merge requests updated on or before the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: If simple, returns the `iid`, URL, title, description, and basic state of merge request

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Returns merge requests for the given scope: `created_by_me`, `assigned_to_me`, `reviews_for_me` or `all`

parameters.name: source_branch
parameters.description: Returns merge requests with the given source branch

parameters.name: source_project_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests with the given source project id

parameters.name: target_branch
parameters.description: Returns merge requests with the given target branch

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search merge requests against their `title` and `description`.

parameters.name: in
parameters.description: Modify the scope of the search attribute. `title`, `description`, or a string joining them with comma.

parameters.name: wip
parameters.description: Filter merge requests against their `wip` status. `yes` to return only draft merge requests, `no` to return non-draft merge requests.

parameters.name: not
parameters.description: Returns merge requests that do not match the parameters supplied

parameters.name: not[author_id]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests created by the given user `id`. Mutually exclusive with `author_username`. Combine with `scope=all` or `scope=assigned_to_me`.

parameters.name: deployed_before

parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests reacted by the authenticated user by the given `emoji`. `None` returns issues not given a reaction. `Any` returns issues given at least one reaction.

parameters.name: not[reviewer_id]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests which have the user as a reviewer with the given user `id`. `None` returns merge requests with no reviewers. `Any` returns merge requests with any reviewer. Mutually exclusive with `reviewer_username`.

parameters.name: deployed_before
parameters.description: Returns merge requests deployed before the given date/time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: deployed_after
parameters.description: Returns merge requests deployed after the given date/time. Expected in ISO 8601 format

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: Returns merge requests deployed to the given environment

parameters.name: approved
parameters.description: Filters merge requests by their `approved` status. `yes` returns only approved merge requests. `no` returns only non-approved merge requests.

parameters.name: merge_user_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests which have been merged by the user with the given user `id`. Mutually exclusive with `merge_user_username`.

parameters.name: merge_user_username
parameters.description: Returns merge requests which have been merged by the user with the given `username`. Mutually exclusive with `merge_user_id`.

parameters.name: approver_ids
parameters.description: Return merge requests which have specified the users with the given IDs as an individual approver

parameters.name: approved_by_ids
parameters.description: Return merge requests which have been approved by the specified users with the given IDs

parameters.name: approved_by_usernames

get.summary: Validates a CI YAML configuration with a namespace
get.description:
Checks if a project’s .gitlab-ci.yml configuration in a given commit (by default HEAD of the
        project’s default branch) is valid

parameters.name: sha
parameters.description: Deprecated: Use content_ref instead

parameters.name: content_ref
parameters.description: The CI/CD configuration content is taken from this commit SHA, branch or tag. Defaults to the HEAD of the project's default branch

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.description: Run pipeline creation simulation, or only do static check. This is false by default

parameters.name: include_jobs
parameters.description:
If the list of jobs that would exist in a static check or pipeline
        simulation should be included in the response. This is false by default

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: Deprecated: Use dry_run_ref instead

parameters.name: dry_run_ref
parameters.description: Branch or tag used as context when executing a dry run. Defaults to the default branch of the project. Only used when dry_run is true

post.summary: Validate a CI YAML configuration with a namespace
post.description: Checks if CI/CD YAML configuration is valid. This endpoint has namespace specific context

get.summary: List of merge requests associated with a deployment
get.description: Retrieves the list of merge requests shipped with a given deployment. This feature was introduced in GitLab 12.7.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or URL-encoded path of the project owned by the authenticated user

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Current page number

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page

parameters.name: deployment_id
parameters.description: The ID of the deployment

parameters.name: author_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created by the given user `id`. Mutually exclusive with `author_username`. Combine with `scope=all` or `scope=assigned_to_me`.

parameters.name: author_username
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created by the given `username`. Mutually exclusive with `author_id`.

parameters.name: assignee_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests assigned to the given user `id`. `None` returns unassigned merge requests. `Any` returns merge requests with an assignee.

parameters.name: assignee_username
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created by the given `username`. Mutually exclusive with `author_id`.

parameters.name: reviewer_username
parameters.description: Returns merge requests which have the user as a reviewer with the given `username`. `None` returns merge requests with no reviewers. `Any` returns merge requests with any reviewer. Mutually exclusive with `reviewer_id`. Introduced in GitLab 13.8.

parameters.name: labels
parameters.description: Returns merge requests matching a comma-separated list of labels. `None` lists all merge requests with no labels. `Any` lists all merge requests with at least one label. Predefined names are case-insensitive.

parameters.name: milestone
parameters.description: Returns merge requests for a specific milestone. `None` returns merge requests with no milestone. `Any` returns merge requests that have an assigned milestone.

parameters.name: my_reaction_emoji
parameters.description: Returns merge requests reacted by the authenticated user by the given `emoji`. `None` returns issues not given a reaction. `Any` returns issues given at least one reaction.

parameters.name: reviewer_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests which have the user as a reviewer with the given user `id`. `None` returns merge requests with no reviewers. `Any` returns merge requests with any reviewer. Mutually exclusive with `reviewer_username`.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Returns `all` merge requests or just those that are `opened`, `closed`, `locked`, or `merged`.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Returns merge requests ordered by `created_at`, `label_priority`, `milestone_due`, `popularity`, `priority`, `title`, `updated_at` or `merged_at` fields. Introduced in GitLab 14.8.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Returns merge requests sorted in `asc` or `desc` order.

parameters.name: with_labels_details
parameters.description: If `true`, response returns more details for each label in labels field: `:name`,`:color`, `:description`, `:description_html`, `:text_color`

parameters.name: with_merge_status_recheck
parameters.description: If `true`, this projection requests (but does not guarantee) that the `merge_status` field be recalculated asynchronously. Introduced in GitLab 13.0.

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created on or after the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Returns merge requests created on or before the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Returns merge requests updated on or after the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: updated_before
parameters.description: Returns merge requests updated on or before the given time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: If simple, returns the `iid`, URL, title, description, and basic state of merge request

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Returns merge requests for the given scope: `created_by_me`, `assigned_to_me`, `reviews_for_me` or `all`

parameters.name: source_branch
parameters.description: Returns merge requests with the given source branch

parameters.name: source_project_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests with the given source project id

parameters.name: target_branch
parameters.description: Returns merge requests with the given target branch

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search merge requests against their `title` and `description`.

parameters.name: in
parameters.description: Modify the scope of the search attribute. `title`, `description`, or a string joining them with comma.

parameters.name: wip
parameters.description: Filter merge requests against their `wip` status. `yes` to return only draft merge requests, `no` to return non-draft merge requests.

parameters.name: not
parameters.description: Returns merge requests that do not match the parameters supplied

parameters.name: not[author_id]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests created by the given user `id`. Mutually exclusive with `author_username`. Combine with `scope=all` or `scope=assigned_to_me`.

parameters.name: not[author_username]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests created by the given `username`. Mutually exclusive with `author_id`.

parameters.name: not[assignee_id]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests assigned to the given user `id`. `None` returns unassigned merge requests. `Any` returns merge requests with an assignee.

parameters.name: not[assignee_username]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests created by the given `username`. Mutually exclusive with `author_id`.

parameters.name: not[reviewer_username]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests which have the user as a reviewer with the given `username`. `None` returns merge requests with no reviewers. `Any` returns merge requests with any reviewer. Mutually exclusive with `reviewer_id`. Introduced in GitLab 13.8.

parameters.name: not[labels]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests matching a comma-separated list of labels. `None` lists all merge requests with no labels. `Any` lists all merge requests with at least one label. Predefined names are case-insensitive.

parameters.name: not[milestone]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests for a specific milestone. `None` returns merge requests with no milestone. `Any` returns merge requests that have an assigned milestone.

parameters.name: not[my_reaction_emoji]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests reacted by the authenticated user by the given `emoji`. `None` returns issues not given a reaction. `Any` returns issues given at least one reaction.

parameters.name: not[reviewer_id]
parameters.description: `<Negated>` Returns merge requests which have the user as a reviewer with the given user `id`. `None` returns merge requests with no reviewers. `Any` returns merge requests with any reviewer. Mutually exclusive with `reviewer_username`.

parameters.name: deployed_before
parameters.description: Returns merge requests deployed before the given date/time. Expected in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: deployed_after
parameters.description: Returns merge requests deployed after the given date/time. Expected in ISO 8601 format

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: Returns merge requests deployed to the given environment

parameters.name: approved
parameters.description: Filters merge requests by their `approved` status. `yes` returns only approved merge requests. `no` returns only non-approved merge requests.

parameters.name: merge_user_id
parameters.description: Returns merge requests which have been merged by the user with the given user `id`. Mutually exclusive with `merge_user_username`.

parameters.name: merge_user_username
parameters.description: Returns merge requests which have been merged by the user with the given `username`. Mutually exclusive with `merge_user_id`.

scopes.api: Grants complete read/write access to the API, including all groups and projects, the container registry, the dependency proxy, and the package registry.
scopes.read_api: Grants read access to the API, including all groups and projects, the container registry, and the package registry.
scopes.read_user: Grants read-only access to your profile through the /user API endpoint, which includes username, public email, and full name. Also grants access to read-only API endpoints under /users.
scopes.create_runner: Grants create access to the runners.
scopes.manage_runner: Grants access to manage the runners.
scopes.k8s_proxy: Grants permission to perform Kubernetes API calls using the agent for Kubernetes.
scopes.self_rotate: Grants permission for token to rotate itself.
scopes.mcp: Grants read-write access to remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for tools execution.
scopes.mcp_orbit: Grants access to the Orbit Knowledge Graph MCP server for graph queries and schema retrieval.

report_data.description:
An object that contains report components mapped to frontend data assets.

Some report formats support embedding UI components from the
UI. For example, users can apply certain filters in the web UI, and
export the component to a PDF. The SVG is provided from
the frontend in the report data field.

Unsupported keys are discarded. If the data
doesn't fit the component it is keyed to, the
component is ignored and an error is logged.

project_vulnerabilities_history.description: This object should include an SVG asset to be rendered in PDF reports

group_vulnerabilities_over_time.description: This object should include an SVG asset to be rendered in PDF reports

project_security_status.description: Data for rendering the project grades summary in PDF reports

total_risk_score.description: Data for rendering the total risk score in PDF reports

open_vulnerabilities_over_time.description: Data for rendering the open vulnerabilities over time in PDF reports

vulnerabilities_by_age.description: Data for rendering the vulnerabilities by age in PDF reports

vulnerabilities_by_severity_count.description: Data for rendering the vulnerabilities by severity count in PDF reports

export_format.description: The format of export to be generated

target_issue_iid.description: The IID of an issue to relate to

target_project_id.description: The ID of the target project

comment.description: Comment why vulnerability was reverted to detected (maximum 50,000 characters).

require_reauthentication_to_approve.description: Should approvers authenticate via password or SAML before adding approval

remove_group.description: Ability to delete or restore a subgroup. This ability does not allow deleting top-level groups. Review the retention period settings to prevent accidental deletion.

admin_compliance_framework.description: Create, read, update, and delete compliance frameworks. Users with this permission can also assign a compliance framework label to a project, and set the default framework of a group.

admin_cicd_variables.description: Create, read, update, and delete CI/CD variables.

manage_deploy_tokens.description: Manage deploy tokens at the group or project level.

manage_group_access_tokens.description: Create, read, update, and delete group access tokens. When creating a token, users with this custom permission must select a role for that token that has the same or fewer permissions as the default role used as the base for the custom role.

admin_group_member.description: Add or remove users in a group, and assign roles to users. When assigning a role, users with this custom permission must select a role that has the same or fewer permissions as the default role used as the base for their custom role.

admin_integrations.description: Create, read, update, and delete integrations with external applications.

manage_merge_request_settings.description: Configure merge request settings at the group or project level. Group actions include managing merge checks and approval settings. Project actions include managing MR configurations, approval rules and settings, and branch targets. In order to enable Suggested reviewers, the "Manage project access tokens" custom permission needs to be enabled.

manage_project_access_tokens.description: Create, read, update, and delete project access tokens. When creating a token, users with this custom permission must select a role for that token that has the same or fewer permissions as the default role used as the base for the custom role.

admin_protected_branch.description: Create, read, update, and delete protected branches for a project.

admin_protected_environments.description: Create, read, update, and delete protected environments

manage_protected_tags.description: Create, read, update, and delete protected tags.

admin_push_rules.description: Configure push rules for repositories at the group or project level.

admin_runners.description: Create, view, edit, and delete group or project Runners. Includes configuring Runner settings.

admin_security_attributes.description: Manage the security categories and attributes belonging to a top-level group. Also requires the `read_security_attribute` permission.

admin_terraform_state.description: Execute terraform commands, lock/unlock terraform state files, and remove file versions.

admin_vulnerability.description: Edit the status, linked issue, and severity of a vulnerability object. Also requires the `read_vulnerability` permission.

read_compliance_dashboard.description: Read compliance capabilities including adherence, violations, and frameworks for groups and projects.

read_admin_cicd.description: Read CI/CD details for runners and jobs in the Admin Area.

read_dependency.description: Allows read-only access to the dependencies and licenses.

read_admin_groups.description: Read group details in the Admin Area.

read_admin_projects.description: Read project details in the Admin Area.

read_code.description: Allows read-only access to the source code in the user interface. Does not allow users to edit or download repository archives, clone or pull repositories, view source code in an IDE, or view merge requests for private projects. You can download individual files because read-only access inherently grants the ability to make a local copy of the file.

read_runners.description: Allows read-only access to group or project runners, including the runner fleet dashboard.

read_security_attribute.description: Allows read-only access to the security categories and attributes belonging to a top-level group.

read_admin_subscription.description: Read subscription details in the Admin area.

read_admin_monitoring.description: Read system information such as background migrations, health checks, and Gitaly in the Admin Area.

read_admin_users.description: Read the user list and user details in the Admin area.

read_vulnerability.description: Read vulnerability reports and security dashboards.

primary.description: Specifying whether this site will be primary. Defaults to false.

enabled.description: Specifying whether this site will be enabled. Defaults to true.

name.description: The unique identifier for the Geo site. Must match `geo_node_name` if it is set in `gitlab.rb`, otherwise it must match `external_url`

url.description: The user-facing URL for the Geo site

internal_url.description: The URL defined on the primary site that secondary site should use to contact it. Returns `url` if not set.

files_max_capacity.description: Control the maximum concurrency of LFS/attachment backfill for this secondary site. Defaults to 10.

repos_max_capacity.description: Control the maximum concurrency of repository backfill for this secondary site. Defaults to 25.

verification_max_capacity.description: Control the maximum concurrency of repository verification for this site. Defaults to 100.

container_repositories_max_capacity.description: Control the maximum concurrency of container repository sync for this site. Defaults to 10.

sync_object_storage.description: Flag indicating if the secondary Geo site will replicate blobs in Object Storage. Defaults to false.

selective_sync_type.description: Limit syncing to only specific groups, or shards. Valid values: `"namespaces"`, `"shards"`, or `null`

selective_sync_shards.description: The repository storages whose projects should be synced, if `selective_sync_type` == `shards`

selective_sync_namespace_ids.description: The IDs of groups that should be synced, if `selective_sync_type` == `namespaces`

selective_sync_organization_ids.description: The IDs of organizations that should be synced, if `selective_sync_type` == `organizations`

minimum_reverification_interval.description: The interval (in days) in which the repository verification is valid. Once expired, it will be reverified. This has no effect when set on a secondary site.

blob_download_timeout.description: Maximum time (in seconds) for blob downloads during Geo sync. Defaults to 28800 (8 hours). Maximum is 86400 (24 hours).

project_id.description: The ID of the project that the runner is created in

maximum_timeout.description: Maximum timeout that limits the amount of time (in seconds) that runners can run jobs

If the value is `null`, the default minimum access level is `maintainer`. Must be provided when `minimum_access_level_for_push` is not set. Behind a feature flag named `packages_protected_packages_delete`. Disabled by default.

minimum_access_level_for_push.description: Minimum GitLab access level required to push a package. Valid values include `null`, `maintainer`, `owner` or `admin`. url.description: The URL to the Jira project which is being linked to this GitLab project (for example, `https://jira.example.com`).

password.description: The Jira API token, password, or personal access token to use with Jira. When using Basic Authentication (`jira_auth_type` is `0`), use an API token for Jira Cloud, and a password for Jira Data Center or Jira Server. For a Jira personal access token (`jira_auth_type` is `1`), use the personal access token.

jira_issue_prefix.description: Prefix to match Jira issue keys.

jira_issue_transition_id.description: The ID of one or more transitions for [custom issue transitions](../integration/jira/issues.md#custom-issue-transitions).Ignored when `jira_issue_transition_automatic` is enabled. Defaults to a blank string,which disables custom transitions.

issues_enabled.description: Enable viewing Jira issues in GitLab.
